items.title: Gas prices could rise above $2/litre across Canada as Iran war drags on, experts say
items.summary_auto: The price of gas crossed the $2 per litre mark in some parts of Canada this week, something the rest of the country could see as the war in Iran drags on, experts say.

items.title: Pam Bondi's loyalty to Trump wasn't enough to keep her job. Here's why
items.summary_auto: For more than a year, Pam Bondi violated the norms of independence expected from a U.S. attorney general and forced Department of Justice attorneys to do the bidding of President Donald Trump. Yet her loyalty and willingness to turn the department into a weapon of retribution for Trump weren't enough to satisfy the president.

items.title: Hegseth tells U.S. Army chief of staff to retire 'effective immediately' amid war in Iran
items.summary_auto: U.S. War Secretary Pete Hegseth has asked the army's top uniformed officer to step down, the Pentagon said Thursday without giving a reason for the departure as the United States wages a war against Iran.

items.title: Is Ukraine about to enter the war in the Middle East? | About That
items.summary_auto: As Iran strikes countries across the Persian Gulf, Ukraine is making defence agreements in the Middle East. Andrew Chang explains how Ukraine’s expertise in fighting Iranian-designed Shahed drones in its war with Russia could reshape the war in the Middle East and help regional allies rethink their defence strategies. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters, Adobe Stock and Getty Images

items.title: Ontario shelter resident says he’d rather return to streets than stay at modular site
Judge dismisses Blake Lively's sexual harassment claim against Justin Baldoni
items.summary_auto: A federal judge has dismissed actor Blake Lively's sexual harassment claims in her lawsuit against actor and director Justin Baldoni, significantly narrowing her case over the filming of their 2024 romantic drama It Ends With Us.

items.title: Northern cod stock is now in the 'healthy zone,' DFO says
items.summary_auto: The province's iconic Northern cod stock appears to be making a comeback.

items.title: Amazon adding fuel surcharge for Canadian, U.S. sellers starting April 17
items.summary_auto: Amazon is applying a fuel surcharge to fulfillment fees for sellers in Canada and the U.S. starting on April 17, a spokesperson for the e-commerce giant confirmed to CBC News on Thursday.

items.title: 'The tribe has spoken’: Survivor fans voice frustration after Trump address interrupts show
items.summary_auto: The Survivor tribe has spoken…. and they’re not thrilled. That’s because on Wednesday night, CBS had to interrupt the series’ extra-long merge episode to air U.S. President Donald Trump’s prime-time address on the war in Iran.

items.title: Ontario announces plan to slash Niagara Region, Simcoe County councils and add 'strong chair' powers
items.summary_auto: Under its new Better Regional Governance Act, 2026, the province will also have the power to appoint regional chairs in Durham, Halton, Muskoka, Peel, Waterloo and York as well as the warden of Simcoe County, it announced Thursday

items.title: Top Canadian bankers join finance minister on Chinese trade mission
items.summary_auto: Representatives from some of the country's most powerful financial institutions have joined a trade mission to China to promote Canada in one of the world's largest markets.

items.title: Quebec passes expanded secularism law targeting daycare workers, public prayer
items.summary_auto: The Coalition Avenir Québec government adopted legislation on Thursday that puts limits on praying in public and extends a ban on wearing religious symbols to daycare workers.

items.title: Trump fires U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump removed Attorney General Pam Bondi from her post on Thursday, a White House official said,  following mounting frustration with her performance, including her handling of investigative files related to the late financier and sex  offender Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: Alberta tables bill to reduce child access to sexually explicit images in public libraries
items.summary_auto: The Alberta government is looking to make changes in all public libraries to ensure children and young teens don't have access to graphic sexual images.

items.title: Gaza family says toddler released from Israeli custody found with cigarette burn marks
items.summary_auto: On March 19, the day before Eid al-Fitr, Osama Abu Nassar took his 18-month-old son, Jawad, to a supermarket east of al-Maghazi refugee camp in central Gaza when eyewitnesses say he approached a restricted area and was detained by the Israeli military.

items.title: 87 kilograms of cocaine seized at Coutts border
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Border Services Agency says it's intercepted 87 kilograms of suspected cocaine from a Calgary-bound commercial truck labelled as carrying produce.

items.title: B.C. mother and daughter released from ICE custody in Texas after being held 19 days
Stellantis talks to build Chinese EVs in Brampton is 'unacceptable': Ontario premier
items.summary_auto: Ontario Premier Doug Ford has reacted to reports that Stellantis is in talks with a Chinese automobile manufacturer about the possibility of building Chinese electric vehicles in Canada at its idled ⁠Brampton assembly plant.

items.title: First Nations protest on 3 N.S. highways as tensions escalate over cannabis raids
items.summary_auto: Sipekne'katik First Nation has blocked one lane of Highway 102 southbound while Millbrook First Nation has blocked one lane northbound after an RCMP raid on a Potlotek First Nation cannabis shop.

items.title: Legault speaks on French in Montreal, Quebec identity in final question period as premier
items.summary_auto: François Legault attended his last question period at the National Assembly Thursday morning before stepping down as premier next week.

items.title: Oil prices up after Trump warns U.S. to hit Iran 'extremely hard' in weeks ahead
items.summary_auto: Oil prices surged on Thursday and equities markets around the world were mixed in volatile trade as traders weighed conflicting developments and remarks related to the Iran ‌war.

items.title: Bogus maple syrup from Quebec producer found on grocery store shelves
Death of near-blind refugee — left at closed Tim Hortons by U.S. Border Patrol — ruled a homicide
items.summary_auto: The death of a nearly blind refugee from Myanmar who was found on a Buffalo, N.Y., street in February — five days after U.S. Border Patrol agents left him outside a Tim Hortons — has been ruled a homicide.

items.title: New Fort St. John doctors to take thousands off waiting list
items.summary_auto: Dr. Manraj Khangura and Dr. Haroon Ahmed are international medical graduates finishing up their two years of residency training in the city. They will take on 3,000 new patients starting in September.

items.title: Workers quit emergency care for Nova Scotia’s most vulnerable after CRA audit
items.summary_auto: A private company that’s received more than $184 million in public funds over the past eight years to care for some of Nova Scotia’s most vulnerable people is now losing workers so quickly, remaining workers say it’s a major safety issue.

items.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake off Indonesia topples buildings, kills 1 person
items.summary_auto: An undersea magnitude 7.4 earthquake toppled buildings in parts of northern Indonesia, sent people fleeing from their homes, killed at least one person and generated a small tsunami Thursday.

items.title: Trial of N.L. man accused of 1st-degree murder delayed over not enough sheriffs to bring him into courtroom
items.summary_auto: In a highly unusual move, Justice Vikas Khaladkar unleashed a wave of criticism when the court was told there weren't enough sheriff's officers to bring Dean Penney, who is accused of murder, to court.

items.title: 25 men are sleeping on cots in the gymnasium of N.L.'s largest prison for the 'foreseeable future'
items.summary_auto: An inmate at Her Majesty's Penitentiary told a judge last week that he's dealing with a leaking roof and a rodent infestation while sleeping on a cot in the jail's gymnasium. The judge toured the facility, and said the gym is "not a very nice place to be living."

items.title: Scammers are targeting Canadians with a milk settlement that doesn't exist
items.summary_auto: At the same time that a phishing scam related to Canada's bread price-fixing settlement is going around, CBC News has learned people are being targeted by a similar scam involving dairy.

items.title: Why mysterious vegetables have been showing up, unordered, on some Ottawans' doorsteps
items.summary_auto: People in Ottawa have been receiving multiple deliveries of vegetables from Walmart that they never asked for. To add further confusion, the deliveries contain only one or two veggies. Giacomo Panico unpacks the mystery.

items.title: What Trump's prime-time TV pitch means for war on Iran
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump is telling Americans that while his objectives for the war against Iran are nearly achieved, he intends to escalate military operations for at least another two weeks.

items.title: What you need to know about the Artemis II moon mission
items.summary_auto: It's an exciting time as four intrepid astronauts — Canadian Space Agency's Jeremy Hansen, along with NASA astronauts, Christina Koch, Reid Wiseman and Victor Glover — are heading to the moon on a 10-day mission. Here's just some of what to expect over the next week.
